factors.name: US Dollar weakness
factors.summary: USD weakened across major pairs on Iran de-escalation hopes (Trump delayed strike), with EUR/USD bouncing to 1.1654 and gold rebounding above $4,550 as noted by FXStreet. Weaker USD is directly bullish for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk de-escalation
factors.summary: Trump paused planned Iran strike at request of Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar; signals 'good chance' of nuclear deal. Oil fell sharply, reducing near-term inflation shock risk, which reduces the case for extreme Fed hawkishness and supports lower real yields.

factors.name: Real yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: No direct TIPS data in this news window, but Bloomberg reports bond yields rose last week amid hawkish Fed Warsh signals and hot inflation data; however Iran pause is easing some of that pressure. Real yields likely still elevated but no fresh print.

factors.name: Fed hawkish pivot (Warsh, inflation focus)
factors.summary: MUFG reports USD posted best week since Iran conflict began as 'hotter-than-expected inflation data, rising yields and arrival of hawkish Fed chair Kevin Warsh' shifted rate expectations higher. This is a structural headwind for gold via higher real yields.

factors.name: Japan GDP beat & BoJ considerations
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP annualized +2.1% (beat 1.7% expected), but price index running hot at 3.4% y/y. Japanese Finance Ministry official sowed doubt on Treasury sales; yen still soft at ~158.80 after six losing days. Mixed implications for yen carry and USD/JPY pressure.

factors.name: Oil price recovery on Iran hopes
factors.summary: Oil futures reopened lower on Iran optimism after Trump pause; near-term supply shock fears easing. Lower energy prices reduce CPI input, supporting case for lower real yields, but also reduces inflation tail-risk premium gold sometimes captures.

factors.name: RBA hawkish inflation warning
factors.summary: RBA Assistant Governor Hunter warns elevated risk of inflation expectations drifting higher due to energy pass-through and capacity constraints. Regional hawkishness doesn't directly move USD or gold, but signals global central banks watching inflation closely.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley equity correction warning
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley warns bond rout could trigger equity correction if volatility persists, though maintains S&P 500 target of 8,300. Risk-off sentiment is typically gold-bullish, but move already underway (stocks closed mixed Monday; S&P, Nasdaq lower).

factors.name: Nominal Treasury yields direction
factors.summary: MUFG notes last week saw largest USD rally since Iran conflict began, driven by rising yields. While geopolitical relief eases acute inflation fears, Warsh-driven hawkish expectations are likely keeping nominal yields supported, a headwind for gold.

factors.name: DXY momentum
factors.summary: USD had best week in weeks last week per MUFG, though easing slightly on Iran news. USD strength is the single largest structural headwind for gold; recent DXY momentum is against gold.

screening.narrative: Gold bounced 0.91% in 24h and 0.50% in 4h on broad USD weakness triggered by Trump's Iran strike pause, which eased acute inflation/energy shock fears and reversed some of last week's hawkish Fed-driven dollar rally. However, the underlying structural headwind remains: Kevin Warsh's arrival as Fed Chair has shifted rate expectations sharply hawkish, gold fell to one-and-a-half-month lows earlier, and nominal yields remain supported despite the geopolitical relief. The bounce appears to be a corrective Phase 2 move into oversold conditions rather than a fresh bull conviction; upside is capped by persistent real yield pressure and dollar strength momentum.
catalysts_ahead: RBA Minutes release (likely hawkish tone given Hunter's comments on inflation risks)
Any further Iran negotiations updates or military action restart (market highly sensitive)
Fed speakers (Warsh, Goolsbee, others) for fresh hawkish/dovish color on inflation focus
US CPI/PCE print in coming weeks (if hotter than expected, crushes gold; cooler = relief)
S&P 500 earnings season and potential correction risk (equity selloff = gold safe-haven bid)
Next FOMC decision and rate path clarity
screening.risk_warning: If Iran negotiations collapse and Trump resumes military action, oil spikes, inflation re-accelerates, and Fed doubles down on hawkish tone, real yields will spike and gold breaks lower. Conversely, if Warsh signals dovish tilt or inflation data cools, gold rallies sharply. Current move is vulnerable to whipsaw; near-term macro clarity is missing.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is Phase 2 (already up 0.91% in 24h); wait for retest of $4,565–$4,580 support before considering fresh longs. Shorts should wait for break of $4,600 resistance on potential hawkish Fed talk to re-establish downtrend; do not chase current bounce.
